target.name: Theseus
target.founder: Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, Sacha Lévy

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Theseus, a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, and Sacha Lévy, is evaluated against a peer universe of 10 private and 2 public comparables in the same sector and adjacent stages. The private peer set includes Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety; public peers trade under DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach here, draws on four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median valuation across this cohort is $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This peer median of $225M serves as the implied valuation under this method.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by single-method availability, yields a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), centered on $225M. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of four peers and the stage heterogeneity within the comp set—three peers are Series A while only one is Seed-stage. A broader application of revenue multiples or DCF methodology would strengthen conviction, pending availability of financial data for the target and expanded peer financials.
